title: chaerophyllum cicutaria subsp. villarsii
intro: Chaerophyllum cicutaria subsp. villarsii, also known as Shepherd's Needle, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it a valuable remedy for treating digestive issues and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Shepherd's Needle include alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, reducing menstrual pain, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and in some cases, it is used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
